steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the base width from the base area
steps.content:
Base area $$= l \times w$$. Given base area $$= 18t$$ cm² and length $$l = \frac{3}{2}$$ cm:
$$18t = \frac{3}{2} \cdot w \implies w = 18t \cdot \frac{2}{3} = 12t \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Set up the surface area formula
steps.content:
A right rectangular prism has six faces forming three pairs. With $$l = \frac{3}{2}$$, $$w = 12t$$, $$h = 10$$:
$$SA = 2lw + 2lh + 2wh$$

steps.title: Compute each pair of faces
steps.content:
• Two bases: $$2lw = 2 \times 18t = 36t$$ cm²
  • Two length-height faces: $$2lh = 2 \times \frac{3}{2} \times 10 = 2 \times 15 = 30$$ cm²
  • Two width-height faces: $$2wh = 2 \times 12t \times 10 = 240t$$ cm²
$$SA = 36t + 30 + 240t = 276t + 30 \text{ cm}^2$$
